q: Architecture is concerned with how features (attributes) are implemented control signals, interfaces, memory technology
Baer defines computer architecture as "the design of the integrated system which provides a useful tool to the programmer"
Based on the picture shown in the video "Birth of a Computer", this group was called "the first programmers"
Computer organization deals with structural relationships that are not visible to the programmer
Computer system refers to the set of attributes of a computer system that are visible to a programmer
In 1950, ENIAC was first created The first general purpose computer
In 1958, integrated circuit or IC was developed
In the 1960s, this is where you find the computer memory
Princeton Institute for Advanced Studies (IAS) computer is the prototype for all subsequent general-purpose computers
The data channel is an independent I/O module with its own processor and instruction set
The first computer was developed in 1964
The IC is a central termination point for data channels, CPU, and memory
This person gave the TedTalk "You should Learn to Program"
Thomas Hobbes said " Arithmetic and Logic are the same things You can do it using Addition and Subtraction"
